description: This schematic diagram (Figure 12) depicts the evolution of non-metallic inclusions during smelting and solidification in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels containing 1 wt%, 3 wt%, and 5 wt% Al. On the left, black faceted and needle-like particles represent single Al₂O₃ inclusions formed during smelting. Along the horizontal "Liquidus" marker, distinct icons denote solidification precipitates: gray hexagonal or polyhedral AlN, gray irregular MnS, and brown angular TiN. Blue arrows link these primary particles to the dashed enclosure on the right, illustrating how early precipitates provide heterogeneous nucleation sites for later ones, yielding composite inclusions. At 1 wt% Al, abundant Al₂O₃ fosters multi-phase aggregates including Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS. At 3 wt% Al, changed solidification conditions produce altered single-particle geometries and composites such as AlN-MnS. The 5 wt% Al row displays diverse isolated single-particle morphologies but no composite-inclusion panel, matching the caption report that Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS composites are absent because of scarce single-particle Al₂O₃. Overall, the figure conveys that increasing Al content shifts the liquidus and solidification path, delays MnS and TiN precipitation, and controls inclusion agglomeration behavior.
